item.title: Full Disclosure: A Third (and Fourth) Azure Sign-In Log Bypass Found
item.description: <p>Invisible password sprays. Invisible logins. Full tokens returned.Nyxgeek here. It&#039;s 2026 and I&#039;ve got two more Azure Entra ID sign-in log bypasses to share with you. Don&#039;t get too excited…these bypasses…</p>

item.title: Building a Detection Foundation: Part 4 - Sysmon
item.description: <p>Filling the Gaps Native Logging Can&#039;tAt this point in our series, we have Windows Security events capturing logon sessions and process creation, and PowerShell logging capturing script execution. That&#039;s a…</p>

item.title: Policy as Code: Stop Writing Policies and Start Compiling Them
item.description: <p>The Problem Nobody Wants to Talk AboutLet me paint a picture most security leaders will recognize.You have 30+ policies living as Word documents on SharePoint. Half of them have filenames like…</p>

item.title: Reduce Repetition and Free up Time With Mobile File Extractor
item.description: <p>If you do the same thing three times, automate it. Introducing Mobile Data Extractor, a Python tool that handles the repetitive work of mobile app data extraction so testers can focus on what matters.</p>

item.title: Building a Detection Foundation: Part 5 - Correlation in Practice
item.description: <p>From Data Sources to DetectionWe&#039;ve covered a lot of ground in this series: Windows Security events for logon tracking and process execution; PowerShell logging for script visibility; Sysmon for network…</p>

item.title: IAM the Captain Now – Hijacking Azure Identity Access
item.description: <p>I decided to spend some research time diving in depth into Identity and Access Management (IAM) within Microsoft Azure. I am going to show you within this blog how IAM permissions can be abused within an Azure…</p>

item.title: Benchmarking Self-Hosted LLMs for Offensive Security
item.description: <p>We put LLMs to the test—let's find out how good AI is at hacking! We walk through six simple challenges with intentionally naïve setups to test how capable each model is at single-step exploit validation.</p>

item.title: Dungeons and Daemons
item.description: <p>Play Roll for Initiative. Hack the Planet.Dungeons &amp; Daemons is a cybersecurity RPG that drops you into the boots of a Red Team operator on a live engagement. Your mission: infiltrate a corporate facility,…</p>

item.title: Mythos, Memory Loss, and the Part InfoSec Keeps Missing
item.description: <p>InfoSec has a bad habit of acting like history started this morning. Something new lands, the industry loses its mind for a week, vendors start talking like the old rules no longer apply, and half the industry suddenly…</p>

item.title: Kerberos with Titanis
item.description: <p>In this article, I’ll walk you through the basics of Kerberos, how to use Titanis for the different parts, and how to mitigate some problems.Titanis SetupI use Titanis tools throughout this article to demonstrate…</p>
